Content_Sketch: You built the smartest thing on Earth.
VO_Draft_EN: You built the smartest thing on Earth.

Content_Sketch: On Friday they fire you from it.
VO_Draft_EN: On Friday, they fire you from it.

Content_Sketch: By Tuesday... you own it again.
VO_Draft_EN: By Tuesday  -  you own it again.

Content_Sketch: You don't fit anywhere. Except online.
VO_Draft_EN: You don't fit anywhere. Not at school. Not at home. The only place that makes sense is a screen. A keyboard. A blinking cursor in a dark room.

Content_Sketch: Systems make more sense than people.
VO_Draft_EN: Systems make more sense than people. You learn that early. Code does what you tell it. People don't.

Content_Sketch: You are nineteen. You drop out.
VO_Draft_EN: You are nineteen. You drop out of the university. Not because you're lost. Because you're bored. Because nothing they're teaching moves fast enough.

Content_Sketch: Your first app sells.
VO_Draft_EN: Your first app sells. Forty-three million dollars. It sounds like a lot. It isn't  -  not for what you actually want.

Content_Sketch: You are twenty-eight.
VO_Draft_EN: You are twenty-eight. You're sitting in a room full of founders  -  hundreds of them  -  pitching ideas, building things. None of them are building what you want.

Content_Sketch: You leave. You build a lab.
VO_Draft_EN: You leave. You start something new. A lab. You tell the whole world what it's for: humanity. The betterment of all humanity.

Content_Sketch: You just never define which one.
VO_Draft_EN: You just never define which one.

Content_Sketch: Top researchers leave million-dollar salaries.
VO_Draft_EN: The best researchers in the world leave their jobs. Million-dollar salaries. Corner offices at the biggest tech companies on the planet. They come to you. Because they believe you.

Content_Sketch: You promise safety. But speed is what gets funded.
VO_Draft_EN: You promise safety. You say it in every interview, every meeting, every keynote. But speed is what gets funded. And you know it.

Content_Sketch: November. You press the button.
VO_Draft_EN: November. You press the button. You release a chatbot. One hundred million people use it in two months. It's the fastest-growing product in the history of the internet.

Content_Sketch: The whole world wakes up.
VO_Draft_EN: The whole world wakes up. Overnight. Everyone's talking about it. Everyone's afraid of it. Everyone wants it.

Content_Sketch: Inside the lab  -  who owns it?
VO_Draft_EN: Inside the lab, no one asks if it's safe anymore. They ask something else. Who owns it?

Content_Sketch: Friday. A message.
VO_Draft_EN: Friday. You get a message from the chief scientist. One line. Meet me.

Content_Sketch: Video call. They don't trust you.
VO_Draft_EN: A video call. Four faces on a screen. We don't trust you anymore. They don't say why.

Content_Sketch: You walk out. Nothing in your hands.
VO_Draft_EN: You walk out of that building. Your backpack over one shoulder. Nothing else. The door closes behind you.

Content_Sketch: 700 people choose you.
VO_Draft_EN: You check your phone. Then again. Then again. Seven hundred people  -  almost everyone who works for you  -  sign a letter. They choose you. Not the board.

Content_Sketch: 48 hours. Loyalty.
VO_Draft_EN: Forty-eight hours. You don't have a company. You don't have a title. You don't have a desk. But you have something more dangerous. Loyalty.

Content_Sketch: Biggest competitor offers. You say no.
VO_Draft_EN: The biggest competitor calls. They offer you a team. Unlimited budget. A fresh start. You say no.

Content_Sketch: Tuesday. You walk back in.
VO_Draft_EN: Tuesday. You walk back into that building. Same door. Same hallway. But this time, no one can make you leave.

Content_Sketch: The old board is gone.
VO_Draft_EN: The old board is gone. Every one of them. The new board  -  you picked them yourself.

Content_Sketch: Worth more than most countries. Still losing money.
VO_Draft_EN: The company is now worth more than most countries. And it's still losing money. Billions. Every quarter.

Content_Sketch: Nonprofit becomes for-profit.
VO_Draft_EN: You convert the nonprofit into a for-profit company. The exact thing everyone warned you about. The exact thing you said would never happen.

Content_Sketch: You control what you said no one should.
VO_Draft_EN: You now control the thing you once said no one should control.

Content_Sketch: No one left to stop you.
VO_Draft_EN: And now there's no one left to stop you.

Content_Sketch: Co-founder sues. Headlines.
VO_Draft_EN: One of the people who helped you start this  -  he sues you. Headlines compare you to fraudsters. Magazine covers. Courtrooms.

Content_Sketch: Saving humanity or selling it?
VO_Draft_EN: The question isn't whether you're right anymore. The question is whether you're saving humanity  -  or selling it.

Content_Sketch: Back to that room. The laptop.
VO_Draft_EN: You're back in that room. The laptop. The screen. The cursor blinking.

Content_Sketch: You built it. Got fired. Took it back.
VO_Draft_EN: You built the smartest thing in the world. It got you fired. You took it back.

Content_Sketch: One question no one can answer.
VO_Draft_EN: But there's one question no one can answer.

Content_Sketch: Did you take control of it... or did it take control of you?
VO_Draft_EN: Did you take control of it  -  or did it take control of you?
